creator: ngie@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon, May 10, 2010 at 7:08 AM, Sylvio C=E9sar <scjamorim@bsd.com.br> wrot=
e:
> 2010/5/9 Garrett Cooper <yaneurabeya@gmail.com>:
>> On Sun, May 9, 2010 at 7:44 AM, Sylvio Cesar Teixeira
>> <sylvio@freebsd.org> wrote:
>>> Hi,
>>>
>>> This problem not happen more. The revision of current currently is: 207=
816
>>>
>>> I had installed vlc in my current version 207816
>>
>> Hi Sylvio!
>> =A0 =A0Please read through the bug log; you'll discover that while this
>> issue is `hidden' because zlib no longer exports those variable
>> declarations, the issue still most likely exists with vlc itself.
>> That's the part that needs to be analyzed and triaged.
>> Thanks,
>> -Garrett
>>
>
> But the important is that the port was installed without problem and work=
 well.

Try telling that to des@ . He was very heated under the collar about
ports being broken with off64_t...

The ports are still broken for all versions of FreeBSD which contain
the off64_t code. Memory serves me correctly, this is just 8.x... but
I could be wrong.

If a bug needs to be filed with vlc, s.t. it's fixed upstream, I'll do
that... but unless the bug is fixed in later versions of vlc, the
problem still exists.

Thanks,
-Garrett
